sections.content:
- Set up Doc Backlog database in Notion (31 tasks across cortex + pm_mvp)
- Scoped cortex v1: indexer + mcp-server only; viz and docjson-extraction are v1.1
- Audited all feature docs — 4 PRDs exist (indexer, mcp-server, viz, doc-manager), all but doc-manager are TODO stubs
- Identified 6 cortex v1 doc tasks: finish indexer PRD + 4 sub-feature PRDs (history, scanning, staleness, test-runner) + finish mcp-server PRD

Planned the viz overhaul across 4 PRDs. Created List View PRD (new), updated Graph View PRD, rewrote Detail Panel PRD, kept Staleness and Scanning PRDs engine-only.

**Branch:** `feature/viz-overhaul`

---

### Post-ADR-009 Alignment (2026-03-16)

ADR-009 (Unified Staleness and Node Types) was merged into master and rebased into this branch. Key changes that affect all remaining phases:

- **Staleness is persisted.** A `staleness TEXT` column on the `nodes` table is the single source of truth. `/api/all` reads it directly (no computation). `/api/check` recomputes via the shared engine in `cortex/index/staleness.py` and persists back.
- **Three divergent staleness implementations collapsed into one.** `_compute_staleness_from_history()`, `_compute_staleness_rich()` (both in viz server), and `_compute_staleness_for_nodes()` (MCP server) are all deleted. `_compute_staleness_for_viz()` is now a thin wrapper around the shared `compute_staleness()` engine.
- **Staleness vocabulary unified.** Six statuses, uniform across all node subtypes:
  - `CONTENT_STALE` — body/main content changed (replaces old `CODE_STALE`)
  - `DESC_STALE` — descriptor/heading changed
  - `LINKED_STALE` — a dependency changed via `documents` or `validates` edge (replaces old `CONTENT_STALE` for docs and `COVERAGE_STALE` for tests). NOT promotable.
  - `STRUCTURAL_DRIFT` — file or node no longer exists. NOT promotable.
  - `VERIFIED` — explicitly confirmed; both hash snapshots match
  - `CLEAN` — in sync
- **Node types reduced to three.** `composite_process`, `atomic_process`, `entity`. The `document` and `constraint` types are removed. Doc sections are `atomic_process` with `subtype=docjson`. Tests are `atomic_process` with a `test` tag.
- **Verification snapshots both hashes.** `node_verification` now stores `code_hash_at` AND `desc_hash_at`. A `desc_hash` change after verification is detected.
- **`build` computes staleness.** The build workflow's final step runs `compute_staleness()` and persists results, so the DB is always current after a build.

Phase 0 and Phase 1 code has been verified correct post-merge — endpoints already read from the persisted staleness column and bulk-verify no longer rejects doc sections.

---

**Phase 0 — Backend endpoints: DONE**

All 4 endpoints implemented in `cortex/viz/server.py`:
- `GET /api/nodes/{id}/staleness-cause` — reads persisted `staleness` column, enriches with current hashes (via AST re-parse for `CONTENT_STALE`/`DESC_STALE`), linked stale nodes (for `LINKED_STALE`), and verification info
- `POST /api/nodes/bulk-verify` — batch verification with per-node ok/error results. No node_type guard — all subtypes (including docjson) are verifiable.
- `GET /api/nodes/{id}/source` — source code with focused line range from `level_3_location`
- `GET /api/nodes/{id}/edges` — inbound/outbound edge list (single hop, no traversal)

Staleness data path: `cortex build` persists staleness → `/api/all` reads column directly → `/api/check` recomputes via shared engine when explicitly requested. Tests: 96 passed.

**Phase 1 — Detail panel tabs: DONE**

Refactored `detail.js` from single-scroll (313 lines) into 5-tab layout (~500 lines). Updated `app.js` selectNode() to fetch staleness-cause in parallel. Added CSS for tab bar, verify form, cause block, edge tables with dark mode overrides.

Tabs implemented:
- **Overview**: badges, ID+copy, location+copy, tags, level_0, staleness cause explanation (from persisted status + server-side enrichment), verify button with reason field, verification attribution
- **Docs**: level_1 ("Level 1 — Description"), level_2 ("Level 2 — Details"), empty state
- **API**: autodoc (params/returns/raises), docstring, merged steps (cortex + dFlow unified), "View Source" stub
- **Relationships**: test coverage, lazy-loaded inbound/outbound edge tables with jump links
- **History**: verification record, rename history, full change history (no 10-item cap)

Key decisions: tab state persists across node select

---

**Phase 2 — List view overhaul: DONE**

Rewrote `list.js` from 84 lines to ~520 lines across two sub-phases. Updated `index.html`, `app.js`, `style.css` with dark-mode overrides. One backend change: added `project_root` to `/api/meta` for VS Code URI construction.

**Phase 2a+2b — Structural + Actions (2026-03-16):**

- **Row grouping engine (US-LV-01):** Client-side grouping by type, subtype, module/file, staleness, or tag. Collapsible group headers with counts. Group-by radio selector in a new list-only sidebar section. Collapsed state persists across re-renders.
- **Clickable location column (US-LV-08):** Location text replaced with `vscode://file/…` links. Constructs absolute path from `project_root` (now exposed in `/api/meta`). Click opens file in VS Code at the correct line.
- **Dedicated list sidebar (US-LV-03):** New "Group By" and "Actions" sections appear only in list view. Existing shared filters remain shared. Sidebar sections toggle via `.list-only-section` class in `setView()`. No file fork — kept inline since TS migration is planned.
- **Staleness cause tooltip (US-LV-07):** Clicking a stale badge fetches `/api/nodes/{id}/staleness-cause` and shows a positioned tooltip with status, cause explanation, linked nodes, and verification info. Results cached per node. Tooltip auto-closes on outside click.
- **Verify per row (US-LV-04):** Checkmark button appears on hover for stale rows. Prompts for optional reason, POSTs to `/api/nodes/{id}/verify`, updates staleness map to VERIFIED and re-renders.
- **Bulk verify (US-LV-04):** Checkbox column with select-all header. Toolbar shows "N of M selected" count. "Verify Selected" button in sidebar POSTs to `/api/nodes/bulk-verify`. Clears selection on success, updates staleness summary.
- **Run Check from list (US-LV-05):** "Run Check" button in list sidebar triggers the same `App.check()` as the header button.

**Phase 2c — Advanced (2026-03-16):**

- **Code preview column (US-LV-02, Tier 1):** Optional "Code" column toggled via toolbar button or column menu. Shows 3-line monospace snippet from the node's focused source range, lazy-loaded from `/api/nodes/{id}/source` with client-side caching.
- **Source panel with Monaco (US-LV-02, Tier 2+3):** "View Source" link on code preview opens a bottom-split Monaco panel. Full file loaded in read-only `vs-dark` theme with minimap. Focus Function toggle switches between full file (with highlighted range) and isolated function view (offset line numbers). Drag-to-resize handle for panel height. Own Monaco instance, independent of Workflow/Tests.
- **Column show/hide (US-LV-09):** Dropdown column menu in toolbar with checkboxes for all 7 columns (select, name, type, location, status, tags, code). Column visibility persists across re-renders. Group header colspan adjusts dynamically.
- **Spawn graph from list (US-LV-06):** "View in Graph" button in sidebar. Uses selected node IDs (or all filtered IDs if none selected), filters `allEdges` to only connecting edges, loads into `Graph.loadAll()`, switches to graph view. Client-side only — no new backend endpoint needed.

No new backend endpoints in 2c — all data comes from existing Phase 0 endpoints. Tests: 119 passed.

Current priorities for the project. Updated the PRDs with AI assistance.

**Recommended order:**

**P0 — Scanner (do first, unblocks everything)**

1. `cortex init` / `cortex build` CLI split — small, high-visibility, sets the right mental model
2. AST call graph `validates` edges — the biggest piece of work, but it's the prerequisite for the test runner being useful at all

**P1 — Staleness surface (parallel with P0 if you want)**

3. Wire CONTENT_STALE into `cortex check` output — completely independent of the call graph work, can be done anytime

**P2 — Report (needs P0 + P1 to be meaningful)**

4. `cortex report` — once call graph edges exist and CONTENT_STALE is surfaced, the report has all three data sources (changed nodes, test results, stale doc sections)

**P3 — Gate (needs P2)**

5. GitHub action — trivial once `cortex report` and `cortex check --fail-on` both work

**P3 (anytime) — Rename detection**

6. Hash-similarity rename — improves data quality but nothing else depends on it; can slot in anywhere

sections.heading: 2026-03-16 — List View Polish
sections.content:
UX refinements to the Phase 2 list view based on hands-on usage feedback.

**Branch:** `feature/viz-overhaul` (continued)

---

**Layout change: 3-column (list | source | detail)**

The bottom-split source panel felt disconnected from the list. Moved to a right-side column layout: clicking a code node opens its source in a middle Monaco column, with the detail panel on the far right. Collapsible via toggle button. For docjson nodes, the source column renders section content as formatted HTML (headings + styled markdown) instead of raw JSON.

Removed the `{ } Code` toolbar button (confusing). "View Source" link now lives inline in the Name column for code nodes — always visible, no column toggle needed.

**Filtering improvements:**

- **Hide private functions by default.** Nodes with `_`-prefixed names are hidden. A "Show private" toggle in the sidebar reveals them. Reduces noise significantly for large codebases.
- **Test path filter from cortex.toml.** `/api/meta` now exposes `test_paths` (e.g. `["tests/"]`). The frontend uses this to filter out nodes whose location starts with a test path, independent of the `test` tag. Toggle: "Show tests".
- **Tags filter redesign.** Replaced the flat toggle pills with a docs-style filter panel: selected tag chips on top, searchable suggestion list below sorted by count, "Show all" overflow. Matches the doc manager UX.

**Location grouping: nested directory tree**

The flat file-path grouping was hard to parse. Replaced with a nested tree: directory rows are collapsible parent groups with indent scaled by depth, file rows are leaf groups containing the actual node rows. Only applies to location grouping — other modes stay flat.

Added **Collapse All / Expand All** toggle to the list toolbar (works for all grouping modes).

sections.heading: 2026-03-18 — History Feature Implementation Plan
sections.content:
History feature implementation plan. Covers the remaining history PRD backlog items across the indexer (Python) and viz (Python server + JS frontend).

**PRD**: `cortex::docs.features.indexer.sub_features.history.prd`
**Design**: `cortex::docs.features.indexer.sub_features.history.design`

---

## Implementation Plan

### Current state (what's already done)

- `node_history` table with append-only rows, `_HISTORY_ROW_LIMIT = 100`
- Content events: `INITIAL`, `CONTENT_ONLY`, `DESC_ONLY`, `CONTENT_AND_DESC` (fired in `upsert_node_conn`) — renamed from `CODE_ONLY`/`CODE_AND_DESC` in Phase 1
- Actor events: `AGENT_VERIFIED`, `MANUAL_VERIFIED` (fired in viz `verify_node`, `bulk_verify`, MCP `cortex_mark_clean`)
- State events: `BECAME_CONTENT_STALE`, `BECAME_DESC_STALE`, `BECAME_LINKED_STALE`, `BECAME_STRUCTURAL_DRIFT`, `BECAME_CLEAN` (fired by `record_staleness` — Phase 2)
- Structural events: `LINK_ADDED`, `LINK_REMOVED` (fired by `cortex_add_link`, `cortex_write_doc`, edge deletion during rebuild — Phase 3)
- `CHECKPOINT` semantic marker (preserved=1) — simplified to marker-only in Phase 4
- `cortex report` CLI + `cortex_report` MCP tool — impact reports since a reference point (Phase 5)
- `cortex_diff` MCP tool + viz diff view — visual and programmatic diff against any history baseline (Phase 6)
- "Since" filter + commit context + scoped diff — Phase 7
- `node_verification` table with hash snapshots
- `cortex_history` MCP tool with stale-window annotations + display for all event types
- `cortex history checkpoint` and `cortex history agent-verified` CLI commands
- Viz history tab showing change history per node with commit subjects
- Staleness engine in `cortex/index/staleness.py` — `record_staleness()` is the unified write point; `compute_staleness()` is the pure computation
- `git_sha` populated in all content-event history rows via `builder.py` → `upsert_node_conn` threading (Phase 1)

### What's missing (from PRD capabilities table)

1. ~~Rename `CODE_ONLY` → `CONTENT_ONLY`, `CODE_AND_DESC` → `CONTENT_AND_DESC`~~ — **Done (Phase 1)**
2. ~~`record_staleness` wrapper with staleness transition events~~ — **Done (Phase 2)**
3. ~~`LINK_ADDED` / `LINK_REMOVED` structural events~~ — **Done (Phase 3)**
4. ~~`cortex report` (impact report from history)~~ — **Done (Phase 5)**
5. ~~VCS change IDs (git SHA) populated in history rows~~ — **Done (Phase 1)**
6. ~~Checkpoint refactor (remove pruning flags — checkpoint should be marker-only)~~ — **Done (Phase 4)**
7. ~~Visual diff for stale nodes (viz source panel)~~ — **Done (Phase 6)**
8. ~~`cortex_diff` MCP tool (structured diff for agents)~~ — **Done (Phase 6)**
9. ~~"Since" filter in viz (list + graph views show only changed nodes)~~ — **Done (Phase 7)**
10. ~~Commit message context in diff view header~~ — **Done (Phase 7)**
11. ~~Scoped diff baseline (diff uses the "since" filter's SHA, not per-node resolution)~~ — **Done (Phase 7)**

**All PRD capabilities are now implemented.**

---

### Phase 1 — Rename change types + git SHA: DONE

**Branch:** `feature/history`

Renamed `CODE_ONLY` → `CONTENT_ONLY` and `CODE_AND_DESC` → `CONTENT_AND_DESC` across all consumers:
- `db.py` `_derive_change_type()`, `get_stale_doc_sections()`, `get_stale_tests()`
- `mcp_server.py` `cortex_history` display logic + stale-window annotations
- `cli.py` `cmd_history_checkpoint` collapsible types
- `detail.js` — data-driven, no hardcoded strings needed

Git SHA threading: `builder.py` calls `get_git_sha()` once at build start and passes it through to every `upsert_node_conn()` call. All content-event history rows now carry the HEAD SHA.

---

### Phase 2 — `record_staleness` wrapper + transition events: DONE

**Branch:** `feature/history`

New functions in `cortex/index/staleness.py`:
- **`record_staleness(db_path, project_root, nodes)`** — unified write point wrapping `compute_staleness()`. In one transaction: reads old `nodes.staleness`, computes new statuses, diffs old vs new, inserts transition events, persists new staleness. Returns same `dict[str, str]` as `compute_staleness()`.
- **`_transition_change_type(old, new)`** — maps staleness transitions to `BECAME_*` change types. Skips no-ops, CLEAN↔VERIFIED interchange, and stale→VERIFIED (covered by actor events).
- **`_get_linked_stale_map(db_path)`** — returns stale-node→triggering-code-node mapping for enriching `BECAME_LINKED_STALE` meta with `linked_node`.

All callers switched from `compute_staleness()` + `persist_staleness()` to `record_staleness()`:
- `cli.py` — `cmd_init`, `cmd_build`, `cmd_check`
- `mcp_server.py` — `_compute_staleness_for_nodes` wrapper → `cortex_build`, `cortex_check`
- `viz/server.py` — `_compute_staleness_for_viz` wrapper → `get_graph`, `get_check`

Display: `cortex_history` MCP tool updated with human-readable descriptions for all five `BECAME_*` event types (with `from` status and `linked_node` in meta).

Dead code: `_compute_staleness_from_history` already removed (confirmed not present in codebase).

Tests: 15 new tests — 10 unit tests for `_transition_change_type` (Tier 1), 5 integration tests for `record_staleness` (Tier 2 with `@workflow(purpose=...)`). Full suite: 145 passed.

---

### Phase 3 — Link events (structural audit trail): DONE

**Branch:** `feature/history`

`LINK_ADDED` and `LINK_REMOVED` events recorded in `node_history` at all three write points:
- `mcp_server.py` `cortex_add_link` — inserts `LINK_ADDED` on the source (section) node after edge creation
- `mcp_server.py` `cortex_write_doc` — inserts `LINK_ADDED` for each `documents` edge from link arrays
- `db.py` edge deletion (two sites: doc node purge and orphan node purge) — inserts `LINK_REMOVED` with `actor: "system"`

Meta format: `{"edge_type": "documents", "source": "<source_id>", "target": "<target_id>", "actor": "agent|system"}`

Display: `cortex_history` MCP tool renders `LINK_ADDED` as `"link added → {target}"` and `LINK_REMOVED` as `"link removed → {target}"`. Viz `detail.js` displays `change_type` as-is (data-driven).

---

### Phase 4 — Checkpoint refactor (cleanup): DONE

**Branch:** `feature/history`

Simplified `cortex history checkpoint` to a pure semantic marker:
- Removed `--keep`, `--older-than`, and `--no-prune` flags
- No row collapsing or deletion — just inserts a preserved CHECKPOINT row with current timestamp + HEAD git SHA
- Supports `--message` / `-m` for optional checkpoint annotation
- `--node-types` filter retained (defaults to `atomic_process,composite_process`)
- The 100-row hard cap in `upsert_node_conn` remains the only pruning mechanism

---

### Phase 5 — `cortex report` (impact report from history): DONE

**Branch:** `feature/history`

New `get_history_since()` query in `cortex/index/db.py`:
- Resolution order: `since_sha` (checkpoint with matching git_sha prefix) → `since_timestamp` (ISO-8601 cutoff) → most recent CHECKPOINT → all history
- Returns all `node_history` rows with `scanned_at > cutoff`, newest-first

New `cortex report` CLI command in `cortex/cli.py`:
- `--since-sha SHA` — match a checkpoint by git SHA prefix
- `--since DATETIME` — ISO-8601 datetime cutoff
- `--format text|json` — output format
- Text output: summary line + categorised sections (content changes, staleness transitions, link changes, verification activity)
- JSON output: structured dict with `summary`, `content_changes`, `staleness_transitions`, `link_changes`, `verifications`
- `@workflow` annotated with Steps for dFlow visibility

New `cortex_report` MCP tool in `cortex/mcp_server.py`:
- Same reference-point resolution as CLI
- `verbose=False` returns summary line only; `verbose=True` includes full breakdown
- Follows existing `cortex_check` pattern (summary + optional detail)

Tests: 8 new tests in `tests/test_report.py`:
- 6 Tier 1 unit tests for `get_history_since()` — checkpoint cutoff, SHA match, timestamp, priority, empty result, no-checkpoint fallback
- 2 Tier 2 integration tests for `cmd_report` — text and JSON output with `@workflow(purpose=...)`

Full suite: 153 passed.

---

### Phase 6 — Diff view (viz + MCP tool): DONE

**Branch:** `feature/history`

New shared helper in `cortex/diff.py`:
- **`get_node_diff(db_path, project_root, node_id, baseline_sha=None)`** — two-tier baseline resolution when `baseline_sha` is not provided: (1) most recent `AGENT_VERIFIED`/`MANUAL_VERIFIED`/`CHECKPOINT` row with non-NULL `git_sha`, (2) fallback to the oldest row with any `git_sha` (typically the `INITIAL` scan — gives "diff since first indexed"). When `baseline_sha` is provided, diffs against that specific commit. Runs `git show {sha}:{file_path}`, slices both old and current content to the node's `level_3_location` line range. Returns `{old_content, new_content, baseline_sha, baseline_date, commit_subject, commit_author, commit_date}`.
- **`_parse_level3(level_3_location)`** — shared parser for `path#L10-L45` format.
- **`_slice_lines(content, start, end)`** — 1-based inclusive line slicer.

Viz endpoint: `GET /api/nodes/{id}/diff?sha=...` in `viz/server.py` — calls `get_node_diff`, optional `sha` query param for explicit baseline. Returns JSON for Monaco diff editor. Error responses are `{error, reason}` dicts handled gracefully by the frontend.

Viz frontend changes:
- **Diff button** in source panel header (next to Focus) — `list.js` adds `_diffMode`, `_diffData`, `_diffEditor` state and `_probeDiff()` to check availability on source open
- **`_renderDiffMonaco()`** — disposes normal editor, creates `monaco.editor.createDiffEditor` with side-by-side view, sets original/modified models
- **`toggleDiff()`** — swaps between normal and diff editors; exits diff mode when Focus is clicked
- **Graceful fallback**: button appears disabled with tooltip reason when no baseline exists
- **Clickable SHA entries** in history tab (`detail.js`) — `detail-sha-btn` buttons call `List.openSourceWithDiff(nodeId, sha)` to open source panel with diff at that specific SHA
- **`openSourceWithDiff(nodeId, sha)`** — fetches diff at the specific SHA via `?sha=` query param and immediately activates diff mode

`cortex_diff` MCP tool in `mcp_server.py`:
- Thin wrapper over `get_node_diff`
- Accepts optional `baseline_sha` parameter to diff against a specific commit
- Returns JSON with `{node_id, baseline_sha, baseline_date, old_content, new_content, summary}` where summary is a line-count delta (e.g. "+5 / -2 lines in body")
- Returns `{"error": "no_baseline", "reason": "..."}` on failure

Tests: 16 tests in `tests/test_diff.py`:
- 6 Tier 1 unit tests for `_parse_level3` and `_slice_lines`
- 8 Tier 1 unit tests for `get_node_diff` — node not found, no baseline, no location, git failure, success with mock git, baseline priority, fallback to oldest SHA, explicit SHA override
- 2 Tier 2 integration tests with `@workflow(purpose=...)` — endpoint fallback and MCP summary

Full suite: 169 passed.

---

### Phase 7 — "Since" filter + commit context + scoped diff: DONE

**Branch:** `feature/history`
**User stories:** US-HI-10 (commit context), US-HI-11 (compare against any point), US-HI-12 ("since" filter)

#### 7a — Backend

**New endpoint:** `GET /api/history/since?sha=...&timestamp=...` in `viz/server.py`:
- Wraps `get_history_since()` + new `resolve_since_cutoff()` from `db.py`
- Returns `{node_ids: [str], baseline_sha: str|null, baseline_timestamp: str|null}`
- SHA resolution: first tries checkpoint match (via `resolve_since_cutoff`); if no checkpoint matches, resolves the SHA as a general git commit via `git log -1 --format=%aI {sha}` and uses its author-date as the cutoff timestamp
- When cutoff is timestamp-only (no checkpoint SHA), resolves nearest git commit via `git rev-list -1 --before={ts} HEAD`

**New endpoint:** `GET /api/history/recent-shas` in `viz/server.py`:
- Returns up to 10 distinct recent SHAs from `node_history` with `commit_subject` resolved via batch `git log --no-walk`
- Used by the sidebar SHA dropdown for quick selection

**New helper:** `_batch_commit_subjects(shas)` in `viz/server.py`:
- Single `git log --no-walk --format=%H %s` call for a list of SHAs
- Returns `{sha: subject}` dict — used by both the recent-shas endpoint and the enriched history endpoint

**New function:** `resolve_since_cutoff(db_path, since_sha, since_timestamp)` in `db.py`:
- Same resolution order as `get_history_since`
- Returns `(cutoff_timestamp, checkpoint_git_sha)` — used by the endpoint to populate baseline info

**Commit context enrichment:** `get_node_diff()` in `diff.py` now runs `git log -1 --format=%s%n%an%n%aI {sha}` after resolving the baseline. Returns `commit_subject`, `commit_author`, `commit_date` in the response dict. Graceful: fields are `None` when git log fails.

**History endpoint enrichment:** `GET /api/nodes/{id}/history` now batch-resolves `commit_subject` for all SHAs in the response via `_batch_commit_subjects()`. Each history row includes `commit_subject` (string or null).

#### 7b — Frontend: "since" filter (sidebar)

**Left sidebar section** (`#since-section`, visible in both list and graph views):
- Preset buttons: "Checkpoint", "24h", "7d"
- Custom input row: SHA/date input + "Go" button
- SHA dropdown: appears on input focus, shows recent SHAs from `/api/history/recent-shas` with commit subjects — click to apply
- Clear button (appears when filter active)
- Status line showing count of changed nodes

**App state additions:** `sinceFilter`, `_sinceBaselineSha`, `_sinceNodeIds` (Set), `_recentShasCache`

**Filtering:** `_applyFilters()` intersects with `_sinceNodeIds` when set. `_applyFiltersAndRefresh()` reloads the graph as a subgraph when since filter is active (uses `Graph.loadAll` with filtered nodes so only changed nodes + edges between them are shown).

**Methods:**
- `App.applySinceFilter(type, customValue)` — fetches `/api/history/since`, stores state, refreshes. When no baseline is resolved (e.g. "Checkpoint" with no checkpoints), shows helpful status message instead of silently returning all nodes.
- `App.clearSinceFilter()` — resets state, restores full index
- `App._loadRecentShas()` — fetches and caches recent SHAs for the dropdown
- `App._renderShaDropdown(shas)` — populates the dropdown UI

#### 7c — Scoped diff baseline

When `_sinceBaselineSha` is set, `List._probeDiff()` passes `?sha={_sinceBaselineSha}` to the diff endpoint — every node in the filtered view diffs against the same reference point. History tab clickable SHAs still override via `openSourceWithDiff(nodeId, clickedSha)`.

#### 7d — Commit message in diff header + history tab

**Diff header:** When diff mode is active and `diff.commit_subject` is present, `_renderDiffMonaco()` renders a `.diff-commit-header` block above the Monaco diff editor with:
- Commit subject (full, no truncation, `pre-wrap`)
- Author + date on a second line (smaller font)
- `max-height: 120px` with overflow scroll for long messages
- Collapses when exiting diff mode (element is rebuilt each time)

**History tab:** `detail.js` `_renderHistoryTab()` shows `commit_subject` inline next to each clickable SHA (`.detail-sha-subject` span, truncated with ellipsis, full subject in tooltip). Subjects come from the enriched `/api/nodes/{id}/history` endpoint.

#### Bug fix: non-checkpoint SHA resolution

The original implementation only matched SHAs against CHECKPOINT rows. When a user selected a general commit SHA (e.g. from `BECAME_CONTENT_STALE` events), the filter silently returned all nodes. Fixed: the `/api/history/since` endpoint now falls back to `git log -1 --format=%aI {sha}` to resolve any git commit's author-date as the cutoff timestamp.

#### Tests

10 new tests in `tests/test_since_filter.py`:
- 5 Tier 1 unit tests for `resolve_since_cutoff()` — SHA match, timestamp passthrough, fallback to latest checkpoint, no checkpoint, SHA priority over timestamp
- 2 Tier 1 unit tests for commit context in `get_node_diff()` — success and graceful failure
- 1 Tier 1 unit test for distinct node_id collapse from history rows
- 2 Tier 2 integration tests with `@workflow(purpose=...)` — endpoint with checkpoint, endpoint with timestamp

Full suite: 179 passed.

---

## Suggested execution order

All phases are complete.

| Phase | Depends on | Status | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 — Rename + git SHA | nothing | **DONE** | P0 (prerequisite) |
| 2 — record_staleness | Phase 1 (clean names) | **DONE** | P0 (core) |
| 3 — Link events | nothing (parallel with 2) | **DONE** | P1 |
| 4 — Checkpoint refactor | nothing (anytime) | **DONE** | P2 |
| 5 — cortex report | Phase 2 + 3 (needs transition + link events) | **DONE** | P1 |
| 6 — Diff view + cortex_diff | Phase 1 (needs git SHA in rows) | **DONE** | P1 |
| 7 — "Since" filter + commit context | Phase 5 + 6 | **DONE** | P1 |

---

### Config Scanner Added (2026-03-19)

Added `cortex/scanners/config_scanner.py` — scans `.claude/` and other config directories for settings, skills, and hook files. Produces `composite_process` nodes with `subtype=config`, whole-file hashing, mtime fast-pass.

**What changed:**
- New scanner: `config_scanner.py` with `scan_config_dir()` entry point
- `builder.py` step 5 calls config scanner for `.claude/` dirs
- `cortex_ontology.yaml`: added `config` as valid `composite_process` subtype
- `graph.js`: added `config: '#f0883e'` to `SUBTYPE_COLORS`
- Tests: 7 test functions in `tests/test_config_scanner.py`

**Docs updated:**
- Architecture, MVP, data model (markdown docs)
- Indexer design, viz design, viz PRD (DocJSON docs)
- This devlog entry
